entry.title: Re: What is alternative solution of Adobe Lifecycle Desig...
entry.content:
I don't think there is any.

by default you have citrix workspace, which gives you access to the windows 11, where the adobe lifecycle designer is installed by default.

entry.title: Re: Task 3 - Enhancing CDS View Entities with Annotations
entry.content:
Hi Sachin,

Thanks for taking part in the challenge. Hope you are better now. Kindly add Search field and search without *

entry.title: Consuming SAP with SAP Build Apps - creating a CRUD application
entry.content:
This blog post is the eighth in a series of posts that cover the connectivity options available for SAP Build Apps to interface with SAP systems. The series will cover connecting SAP Build Apps with the following:

  1. CAP-based services
  2. S/4HANA systems
  3. ABAP systems

The format of this post is similar to a tutorial. However, I'll provide more comprehensive details, tips and the opportunity for you to provide feedback. Based on the feedback we will be able to adjust and enhance this post and future parts of the series, and might even be able to improve the products involved as well. 

Introduction 

Before we get into the details of setting up connections between SAP Build Apps and SAP Systems, I intend to introduce a couple of SAP technology components in each post that are relevant for the use cases covered in the series.

CRUD operations

The acronym CRUD stands for Create, Read, Update, Delete. CRUD operations refer to the four basic functions of persistent storage. In the context of OData (Open Data Protocol), these operations are used to interact with data services.

Take note that when interacting with a data service, you are not only storing or retrieving data. The service can also implement (business) logic that is executed when receiving such requests. E.g. for data validation, or triggering another process. Besides that, when looking at access control discussed in part 2 of this blog series, we can configure access to these operations individually for each user.

Use case H : creating a CRUD application in SAP Build Apps

The application we have built so far in this series only shows lists of information. There is no way for the user to create new records, update existing records, or delete records. In this blog post we will change the application we've built in SAP Build Apps into a so-called CRUD application, allowing users to make change to the data stored in the database.

Assuming we are starting a new development session, we open the SAP Build lobby.

As visible in the list of SAP Build projects, we now have 2 versions of the UI application created in SAP Build Apps. Let's open this by selecting the link.

SAP Build Apps opens, and notifies us that there are updates in the marketplace. This is due to a recent release of updates that include components released in the marketplace.

Clicking on the notification brings us to marketplace updates.

Select INSTALL UPDATES to retrieve the latest updates for the flow functions covering create, update and delete a record and SAVE your updated project. These updates are actually quite relevant for this use case!

We can test the current app in Preview to see whether these changes had any impact on our app. When selecting Preview, you will be able to pick the version. We'll pick the latest.

Just as to showcase the starting point of our development, here is a screenshot of the current web preview. It is a very basic list without pagination.

Notice that I've deployed some more initial sample data for the service created in SAP Build Code. This will help while testing the page, for which will also implement the data pagination.

Update the data entity used in our project

Before we get started, we need to update our project to the latest changes in the data service. When we started developing the application, the data entity Products only contained the properties ID, name and price. In the previous blog post we have added a relationship to Supplier, which is stored in a S/4HANA Cloud system.

To update the project, simply navigate to the DATA tab in SAP Build Apps and select the OData integration for Products. The Data entities overview will show the additional field a_Supplier_Supplier, which we've added. However, this simply reflects the current information retrieved from the data service. This might change over time, as new versions of the service are released. Your project in SAP Build Apps does not automatically update to reflect these changes.

To update your project, select DISABLE DATA ENTITY, followed by ENABLE DATA ENTITY for the data entity Products, to ensure the newly added property Supplier is included in your project.

 

Creating a new page to replace the existing one

Instead of changing the existing starting page of the app, we will start by creating a new one. This allows you to refer to a fully functioning page, while we create a replacement. In case you misconfigured something, you can easily check the old page.

Navigate to the pages overview and select ADD NEW PAGE. Provide a name for the page. In this case, I am assigning it the name "Products List", which is going to replace the page "Blog Demo App".

Once the UI canvas editor opens, switch to the VARIABLES and navigate to the DATA VARIABLES tab. Select ADD DATA VARIABLE and pick the Products entity. Rename the data variable to ProductsList. Open the logic editor and connect the Page focused event to the Get record collection. Whenever a user navigates to this page, we will refresh the record collection. This ensures we see the latest updates reflected in the list.


We also add Page Variables to handle the pagination. This will be similar to what we created in blog episode 4, but we'll use a different UI control here and a slightly different configuration.


Adding the list control and page navigation

Switching back to UI Canvas View, we will add list item, navigation buttons and a text field to indicate the page number.  

To prevent the user from navigating to invalid pages, we'll make use of the Disabled property on the buttons. For the left button, we control the property with the following formula.

 

IF(pageVars.CurrentPage>1, false, true)

 

For the right button, we'll disable it to prevent the user from navigating beyond the last page. For this, configure the Disabled property and bind it to the formula:

 

IF(pageVars.CurrentPage<pageVars.TotalPageCount, false, true)

 

For the displaying the current and total amount of pages, we simply bind the Content property to the formula

 

"Page: "+pageVars.CurrentPage + " of "+pageVars.TotalPageCount

 


We'll need to add some logic to both buttons to increase or decrease the current page number respectively. Select the button and open the logic canvas.


For the left button, we set the page variable CurrentPage as follows:

 

pageVars.CurrentPage-1

 

Since we disable the button once CurrentPage is 1, we can keep this formula simple.

For the right button, we set the page variable CurrentPage as follows:

 

pageVars.CurrentPage+1

 


After updating the CurrentPage variable, we configure "Get record collection" with pagination as below.

And after this, we will set the data variable ProductsList to the Output value of the previous node.

We apply the above for the both buttons.


Getting the total amount of records

The page variable TotalPageCount is based on the total amount of records in the database divided by the amount of items we display per page. In this example we will use a fixed number of 10 items per page. You could implement more sophisticated paging that fills the visible screen or window, but we'll keep it simple here.


Select the page canvas and open the logic editor. We will get the record collection of Products, followed by setting the page variable TotalPageCount as follows:

 

INTEGER((outputs["Get record collection"].totalCount -1) / 10) + 1

 

The formula uses the output of "Get record collection" to find out the total amount of records. To prevent loading all data, adding unnecessary overhead and even potential crashes, we need to customise this data request. We configure the property Paging of Get record collection. For the binding, select Object with properties, configured as shown below.

Since we don't need to load any data to retrieve the total record count, we will set the page size and page number to zero. What's relevant for now, is to Include total count.

Switch back to the VARIABLES view and update the logic flow of ProductsList. For Get record collection, we configure Paging.

And we store the result of Get record collection in the ProductsList variable.

 

Adding the new page into the application's navigation flow

Now that we are done with the list and the pagination, we add the page into the application's navigation flow. Go to the NAVIGATION tab and select Add item. Select the page we have developed. Give it a meaningful name and assign it a suitable icon.

 

Previewing and testing the updated app

Navigate to LAUNCH and select Open preview portal. Select Open web preview and use the latest version to Open preview. If all is configured correctly, we should see a list of 10 products and are able to use the buttons to navigate the pages.

 

Adding Create, Update and Delete

In the previous section, we have covered the Read operations for this CRUD app. It is now time to add the functionality for Create, Update and Delete.

Navigate to the pages overview and select ADD NEW PAGE. Provide a name for the page. In this case, I am assigning it the name "Create new product".

Once the page is opened in UI Canvas editor, switch to the variables and create a new data variable for storing the new record. Add a new data variable based on Products. As for the data variable type, select New data record. For the name of the data variable, I am using "newProduct".

On the UI Canvas, drag and drop a Button and a few Input fields. Bind the value of the input fields, to the newProduct record items. 

For the Create button, we need to add some more logic.

On receiving the component tap event, we create a new data record in Products. Upon success, we briefly show a toast message and navigate back to the previous page. Upon failure, we will show an alert and stay on the current page.

As for the configuration of Create record, we use the following custom object:

For ID, we will generate UUID using a formula. The properties name and price are assigned the corresponding values in the newProduct record.

Adding a detail page for Read, Update and Delete

Following the master-detail UI pattern, we will now add a new page that shows the details of a selected record.

Navigate to the pages overview and select ADD NEW PAGE. Provide a name for the page. In this case, I am assigning it the name "Product Details".

Once the page is opened in UI Canvas editor, switch to the variables and create a new data variable for the storing the selected record.

We add a page parameter called productID of type UUID.

Add a new data variable based on Products. As for the data variable type, select New data record. For the name of the data variable, I am using "selectedProduct", and the ID will be the previously created productID.

Going back to the UI canvas, we drag and drop a few buttons and input fields.

For the Delete button, we add some more logic. When the Delete button is pressed, we ask for confirmation using a dialog. If confirmed, we delete the record with ID productID from Products. When successful, we navigate back to the previous page. If deletion fails, we show an alert to inform the user.

For the Update button, we add a similar logic flow. When the Update button is pressed, we update the record with ID productID of Products. When successful, we navigate back to the previous page. If the update fails, we show an alert to inform the user.

Now that we are done with the Product Details page, we will configure the page navigation for accessing the new pages we have added to the app.

Adding page navigation

We go back to the newly created Products List page, and drag and drop a button onto the page. I've also removed the Title and the Text on top of the page to make room for a button.

Upon receiving the Component tap event, we open the 'Create new product' page, and provide the selected item as parameter.

 

After testing the app in preview, and are happy with the new page, we can remove the old Products List and replace it with the new one. We also rename the new page and move it to the top of the navigation list.


Resolving an interoperability issue between SAP Build Apps and CAP version 8

At the time of writing this blog post, we have identified an issue with OData create and update operations initiated from SAP Build Apps. The data service based on CAP CDS version 8 does no longer support the property odata.type and reports error 400 if you do try to use it. We are working to resolve this issue, while making sure all other integrations keep working as expected.

If you run into this issue, you can opt for using the old protocol adapter in your data service created in SAP Build Coce by configuring the following:

 

cds.features.odata_new_adapter = false

 

As a temporary workaround, you can add the following in your project's package.json file:

Once you redeploy your data service, the create and update operations will work again.
More information on the use of protocol adaptors in CAP can be found in the CAP documentation. 

Not the most efficient implementation

Is this the most perfect and efficient configuration of a CRUD app in SAP Build Apps? Not likely. That was not the goal of this blog post. I have tried to keep things simple. There is surely room for improvement, such as:

  • Trigger less OData service requests
  • Make use of entity data already received, instead of sending another query
  • Faster trigger of queries when navigating pages

Stay tuned for improvements in this area, while we are adding more features to SAP Build Apps.

The end-result on Mobile (preview app)

Below is a screen recording of the app running on my iPhone inside the SAP Build Apps app. I am showing a list of products (Read), Adding a new product (Create), checking whether the pagination counts the correct number of pages, update to price of a selected product (Update) and finally remove a selected product (Delete).

Summary – use case G

In this use case we have replaced a simple list view page with a CRUD page. Instead of modifying the existing page, we've introduced a new one, that eventually replaced the original page.

As a first step, we've added pagination. This avoids loading lot's of data coming from an S/4HANA Cloud system. 

Next, we've added a page for adding new products and add a button to navigate to this page.

Follow the master-detail UI pattern, when a user selects a product from the list, a new page will open that shows all the details of the product. In our example we have Supplier as additional information. But there is surely room for more. The detail page allows users to update the record. We've also added a button to delete the record.

What's next

In the next blog post, we will continue on the connectivity topic and move our focus to connecting to ABAP systems.

entry.title: Re: Unable to query/filter LastChangeDateTime in S/4 !
entry.content: Thanks Dinu. It worked

entry.title: renumber g/l account
entry.content:
Dear experts,

We are planning to renumber the g/l accounts. But our customer required a reference field to store the previous g/l numbers for reference. Does s4hana cloud public edition have a standard reference field for us to use? Or we need to create custom field?

entry.title: Re: RFC Definition Errors
entry.content: Check if there are string type in the structures defined. RFC don't support using string in tables parameter.

entry.title: Re: Task 4 - Implement Criticality feature and create an Object page
entry.content:
Hi Bharath,

To display the text instead of key, you need to use annotation ValueHelpDefinition to the Status key field (O,A,X).

To get the criticality use UI.Identification to the Status field which would display the text. You can hide the Status text field.

entry.title: Re: The order of payload when using Odata adapter in SAP CPI
entry.content:
Hello Ryan,

Thank you for your answer.

But the correct order is Product, ProductType, LastChangeDate.

However, what I get in payload is 

<A_ProductType>

<LastChangeDate>2024-01-10T00:00:00.000</LastChangeDate>

<ProductType>SERV</ProductType>

<Product>A001</Product>

</A_ProductType>

As you can see, the order is all reversed.

Or am I interpretating the payload wrongly?

entry.title: Re: S/4HANA service - requote a service quation with refe...
entry.content:
Hello Vojta,

Thanks for your question. I am able to follow exactly your description of the process. Yes this is the expected behavior and it is working correctly as designed.

1. The subitems are never taken over to any follow-up document. It is not taken over into service order, it is not taken over into follow-up / copied quote and it is not taken to requote.

2. The maintenance order linkage is already transferred to service order. This linkage is not carried over into requote.

The perception is that these are purely representing cost line items and helping to evaluate the planned cost and margins. Other than this, these lines have no meaning, no lifecycle of their own. Hence they are not relevant in any follow-up document.

Please could you elaborate your purpose in more detail ? Why do you think the maintenance order and these subitems should be carried over in requote ? Please note that price items can still be added without any issues.

Thanks and Kind Regards,

Abhishek

entry.title: Re: API Error G/L Accounting Document
entry.content:
I found this to be the solution.
Thank you for your help.

解決済み: ジャーナル エントリ - ポスト (同期): エントリが再入力されました - SAP Community

entry.title: Re: Better way to extract all the details from SAP BO instead of calling each object with ID?
entry.content: Hi Dell, can you please share the program you are referring to. We need similar thing for one of the our migration project for SAP BO. We need to extract all the metadata. Any advice on this will be highly appreciated.

entry.title: Re: BRIM solution : Convergent Charging product release strategy?
entry.content:
Hello Sebastien,

I have been searching for any official information from SAP on the release cycle of Convergent Charging. You state that it aligns with the S/4HANA release cycle which suggests that late in 2025 there will be a Convergent Charging 2025. Do you know where I might find this?
Regards,

David

entry.title: Re: SAP metadata export
entry.content: Thank you, we are actually running a migration project for SAP BO. We need these metadata for that purpose. I am new to this so do you suggest any other better approach for this project?

entry.title: Re: Some Business partners not visible while using exchan...
entry.content:
Hi,

There are various things which can be checked to identify the cause of missing BP in ERD:

1. Check and confirm if BP which is not appearing is set to Inactive. Check on COPY by activating the same.

2. Check if the 'Consider Recon. Date' is marked in selection criteria. If marked and BP not appearing check what is the status when checkbox is not marked.

3. When 'Consider Recon. Date' is marked, check if for the BP, user has manually entered a JE for the Exchange Rate amount or not.

Kr,

Jitin

entry.title: Re: Mismatch value between Inventory audit report V/S Inv...
entry.content:
Hi, 

I have single item use only in new test database and only make the one transaction related inventory.

entry.title: application to CF
entry.content:
hi 

 

deployed app to CF and it is up and runing  and i can access the url. https://snow.internal.cfapps.eu12.hana.ondemand.com/ webpage. when i pass any prompt within the page it throws below error but it works after i restart the app and it works without any problem and i face same problem for every 24 hours. restart helps everytime. how to fix this permanent

Internal Server Error

The server encountered an internal error and was unable to complete your request. Either the server is overloaded or there is an error in the application.

entry.title: Re: Create Menu Button/Action within a Section
entry.content: Hey, did you get a solution on this topic? We are facing the same issue. Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Reverse kitting without BOM gives error - Wrong stock...
entry.content: This is now solved. The Warehouse process type KTRI that is used for this process had the availability group 001 as it was copied from sample warehouse 0001.

entry.title: Driving Sustainability Through Process Optimization and Process Mining
entry.content:
In an era where environmental responsibility is no longer just a corporate social responsibility (CSR) but a critical business imperative, sustainability and circular design have emerged as key strategies for organizations across industries. These approaches not only address environmental concerns but also drive efficiency, innovation, and long-term profitability. At the heart of enabling these strategies are advanced process optimization and process mining solutions, which provide the insights and tools necessary to transform traditional linear processes into sustainable, circular ones.

 

The Growing Importance of Sustainability and Circular Design

Sustainability has become a cornerstone of modern business strategy, driven by growing regulatory pressures, consumer demand for eco-friendly products, and the urgent need to combat climate change. Circular design, a critical component of sustainability, focuses on creating closed-loop systems where products and materials are reused, refurbished, or recycled, minimizing waste and resource consumption.

In contrast to the traditional "take-make-dispose" linear economy, circular design aims to extend the lifecycle of products, reduce the need for virgin materials, and ultimately create a regenerative system that benefits both the environment and the economy.

Process Optimization: The Catalyst for Sustainable Transformation

Process optimization is the practice of refining and improving business processes to achieve greater efficiency, reduce costs, and enhance overall performance. In the context of sustainability, process optimization plays a crucial role in minimizing resource consumption, reducing waste, and lowering the environmental impact of business operations.

Key Areas of Impact:

1. Resource Efficiency: By optimizing processes, organizations can significantly reduce the use of raw materials, energy, and water. This not only decreases costs but also reduces the environmental footprint of production activities.

2. Waste Reduction: Process optimization can identify and eliminate inefficiencies that lead to waste, whether it's excess materials, time, or energy. Leaner processes mean less waste and a more sustainable operation.

3. Energy Management: Optimized processes often lead to better energy management, with reduced consumption and lower greenhouse gas emissions. This is particularly important in energy-intensive industries like manufacturing.

4. Product Lifecycle Management: Through process optimization, companies can extend the lifecycle of their products, making them more durable, easier to repair, and more recyclable, thereby supporting circular design principles.

Process Mining: Uncovering Opportunities for Circularity

Process mining is a data-driven approach to analyzing and improving business processes. By extracting and analyzing data from IT systems, process mining provides a detailed, real-time view of how processes actually operate, identifying bottlenecks, inefficiencies, and deviations from the ideal process flow.

When applied to sustainability and circular design, process mining offers several powerful benefits:

1. Transparency and Visibility: Process mining provides unprecedented visibility into the entire lifecycle of a product, from raw material sourcing to production, distribution, and end-of-life. This transparency is essential for identifying opportunities to reduce waste and increase resource efficiency.

2. Data-Driven Decision Making: With process mining, organizations can make informed decisions based on actual process data rather than assumptions. This enables targeted interventions that have the greatest impact on sustainability goals.

3. Continuous Improvement: Process mining supports continuous improvement by enabling organizations to monitor processes in real-time and make adjustments as needed. This iterative approach is key to achieving long-term sustainability and maintaining a competitive edge.

4. Compliance and Reporting: In an increasingly regulated environment, process mining can help ensure compliance with environmental standards and regulations. It also simplifies the reporting process by providing accurate, up-to-date data on process performance and sustainability metrics.

Integrating Process Optimization and Process Mining for Circular Design

To fully realize the benefits of sustainability and circular design, organizations need to integrate process optimization and process mining into their business strategies. Here’s how:

1. End-to-End Process Mapping: Start by mapping out the entire value chain, from raw material sourcing to product disposal. Use process mining to gather data on current operations and identify areas where circular design principles can be implemented.

2. Identify Key Metrics: Establish key performance indicators (KPIs) related to sustainability, such as carbon footprint, water usage, waste generation, and recycling rates. Use these metrics to guide process optimization efforts.

3. Implement Circular Design Initiatives: Redesign processes to incorporate circular design principles, such as modular product design, material recycling, and product take-back programs. Use process optimization to ensure these initiatives are implemented efficiently and effectively.

4. Monitor and Adjust: Continuously monitor process performance using process mining tools. Make data-driven adjustments to optimize processes further and achieve sustainability goals.

5. Scale and Replicate: Once optimized processes are in place, scale these practices across the organization and replicate successful initiatives in other areas or regions.

Conclusion: A Pathway to Sustainable Success

Incorporating sustainability and circular design into business operations is no longer optional; it is essential for long-term success. By leveraging process optimization and process mining solutions, organizations can transform their traditional linear processes into sustainable, circular ones that minimize environmental impact while maximizing efficiency and profitability.

As businesses continue to face increasing pressure to operate sustainably, those that embrace these technologies will be better positioned to lead in the emerging circular economy, ensuring their long-term viability and contributing positively to the planet.

entry.title: Re: UDF Setup history item list BP ordered
entry.content:
Hi,

there were a small typo in the query, try

SELECT
	[RDR1].[ItemCode]
	,SUM([RDR1].[Quantity]) AS [Sum]
FROM
	[ORDR]
	INNER JOIN [RDR1] ON [RDR1].[DocEntry] = [ORDR].[DocEntry] 
WHERE
	[ORDR].[CardCode] = $[$4.0.0]
	AND
	[ORDR].[DocDate] Between DATEADD(dd,-180,GetDate()) AND GetDate() 
GROUP BY 
	[RDR1].[ItemCode]

Save it

Go to your desired Field press Shift+Alt+F2

Place the query.

Close the form, open it again. Choose a BP. Press Shift + F2 in the field

If the query returns only one line it fills the field automatically.

regards Lothar

entry.title: Re: Sales Commissions - External Sales Representative (2TT)
entry.content:
Hi Carlos_H_Rodriguz

Thank you for your prompt reply.

entry.title: Uploaded .PDF file by DTW in OCRD Table in SAP B1
entry.content:
Dear All,

I want to upload the all Vendors MSME Certificate file as a attachment by DTW. Can anyone suggest, how it will work?

entry.title: Re: Batch Determination Setup Scenario in S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
How we can implement this senerio where the during batch determination of Sales delivery material with shelf life less than 75 % should not be picked. 

Please suggest

entry.title: Re: In-app purchases
entry.content:
You can use stripe for physical goods, but services to upgrade features and subscriptions need to use in app purchasing. And you can't redirect or give a link to a payment page from within the app. However, when a user hits a limit or might upgrade it seems you can email them a link to complete the purchase using your own payment method. This isn't as seamless for the user so it depends on your app if it is worth it knowing the conversions would be less because of the extra step (assuming you have their email addresses in the first place)

The complexity and hassle of dealing with in-app payments has resulted companies like RevenueCat  facilitating the process.  I have not used them or any other service like it. If you find something a solution that integrates easily with build apps please let me know.

entry.title: Re: Authentication issues when using RESTful api C# for 4...
entry.content:
Hi ayman_salem,
Thanks for reply.

The baseurl is in the form of http://<server>:6405/biprws.

I had added this syntax previously but didn't see a change:

using (System.Net.Http.HttpClient client = new System.Net.Http.HttpClient())
{
	// Add the Accept header to the request
    client.DefaultRequestHeaders.Accept.Add(new System.Net.Http.Headers.MediaTypeWithQualityHeaderValue("application/xml"));
// code continued here...

 This is the reponses I get both when hardcoding the credentials and baseurl and with variables :

responseStatusCode: 401, ReasonPhrase: 'Unauthorized', Version: 1.1, Content: System.Net.Http.HttpConnectionResponseContent, Headers:
{
  Server: Apache-Coyote/1.1
  Access-Control-Allow-Credentials: true
  Access-Control-Allow-Origin: http://<servername>:8080
  Access-Control-Expose-Headers: X-SAP-LogonToken, X-SAP-PVL, WWW-Authenticate
  Date: Tue, 03 Sep 2024 05:22:23 GMT
  Content-Type: application/xml
  Content-Length: 431
}

or

responseStatusCode: 401, ReasonPhrase: 'Unauthorized', Version: 1.1, Content: System.Net.Http.HttpConnectionResponseContent, Headers:
{
  Server: Apache-Coyote/1.1
  Date: Tue, 03 Sep 2024 05:30:13 GMT
  Content-Type: application/xml
  Content-Length: 431
}

The url to get the response works so assume the api is accepting requests

http://servername:6405/biprws/logon/long

Is there a log file I can check for more debug information. I wonder if the requestBody syntax is not correct.

entry.title: Re: differnce between smartform and adobe form
entry.content: Smart Forms are more straightforward and integrated with SAP, ideal for standard forms with less complex design requirements. Adobe Forms offer advanced design features and flexibility, suitable for complex forms with intricate layouts and design needs.

entry.title: Re: differnce between smartform and adobe form
entry.content:
• Smart Forms are more straightforward and integrated with SAP, ideal for standard forms with less complex design requirements.
  • Adobe Forms offer advanced design features and flexibility, suitable for complex forms with intricate layouts and design needs.

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Dear @David_Chaviano ,

I am an SAP Business One Consultant from Pakistan, with a background in Computer Sciences and Business Administration. I am keen to further expand my skills and knowledge within the SAP ecosystem.

I have recently completed my learning journey relevant to SAP S/4 HANA Public Cloud certification, as reflected in the attached screenshots. The digital skills initiative by SAP Learning presents a fantastic opportunity for individuals like myself, and I am eager to take on the challenge and undergo the necessary training to achieve SAP Certification.

Thank you for considering my interest in this initiative. I look forward to the possibility of participating in the program.

entry.title: Re: Using location functions and variables
entry.content: There's a  formula to determine this... I suppose as a last resort you can ask Chatgpt or Gemini to write it in Javascript for you and then use the custom Javascript flow function.

entry.title: How to link new bank account to an entity in SAP BYD?
entry.content: I have created a new bank ledger in the chart of the accounts and have linked it to an Accounts Determination Group. I want to know how can I link this bank ledger to a specific entity in BYD and start posting the entries?

entry.title: Re: Which sales doc type & item category should I use for...
entry.content:
Hi Sumi_malik303 

In the SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition, we only have a standard order (OR). Currently, we can’t define new order types or item categories in the system. We have to use standard configuration only. 

Regarding your question, yes, we do have an order-related billing item category. You can use CBAO (relevant for billing plan) if you don’t want to do sales of service. Also, we need to change the item category manually while creating a sales order, but if you don’t want to do this activity manually, please refer to the link below to understand flexible item category determination

Flexible Item Category Determination 

Thank You

entry.title: Re: IA38 Mass change not working
entry.content: Thanks for your answer. I will check it with our SAP admin when he is back from holiday

entry.title: Re: 🟣 Transform Data with Scripting in SAP Build Process Automation
entry.content: I am told that it will return very soon ... and be expected by end of Q3 (still, no guarantees)

Thanks

entry.title: calculate GL cumulative balance in GR55 report
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

Please suggest the formula to calculate cumulative GL balance available in FAGLB03 in custom built GR55 report.

Thanks

entry.title: Alternative options of Group reporting-1SG in SAP S/4 public cloud
entry.content:
Dear experts,

My client has a requirement to post elimination entries for consolidation purpose (within same geographical region) hence they need to set up consolidation elimination company code for this purpose in S/4 HANA public cloud.

I know this requirement can be met by activating 1SG scope item (Group reporting- Financial Consolidation) that incurs additional license costs, but I would like to know if this can be achieved without 1SG and what are the alternatives/considerations for the same. Anyone has experienced this kind of requirement in their projects on S/4 public cloud, please throw some insights.

Appreciate a quick advise on this.

Thanks!

SAP S/4HANA Finance SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Finance

entry.title: Re: ABAP class/FM/BAPI/IDOC to create/maintain Inbound De...
entry.content:
Hi @dhawal_patil ,

Please see below links how to manage transaction level change from program. 

https://d.dam.sap.com/a/LLzDfjK/EWM_Transaction_Handling.pdf?rc=10

Example-Program - /SCWM/R_DLV_ADJUST (See how data read and update)

Class- /scdl/cl_sp_prd_inb or /scdl/cl_sp_prd and method inset for new item... See above program as an example.

Also Check your functional Consultant and ask - to show the process (create the inbound delivery manually in SAP EWM).Thats how you build the things in program...

Please let us if you have further questions...

Kind Regards,

Ajit

entry.title: 如何在BAS中创建包含DB，SRV，Fiori的CAP工程
entry.content:
在SAP BTP环境中，SAP更推荐使用SAP Business Application Studio作为开发环境以及SAP Cloud Application Programming Model作为开发框架发，其中BAS提供了基本的开发测试部署环境，CAP框架涵盖了从DB到OData Service到Fiori的整体开发方案。

在SAP提供了多种创建CAP工程并且添加module的方法，但是在创建CAP工程的过程中，不同的选项都会影响工程配置参数。对于初学CAP开发的人员，经常因为对于创建工程的选项不熟悉，通过不同方式的创建工程，参数配置文件不完整，导致程序无法打包或者部署失败等。由于配置文件过于复杂，对于初学者很难发现其中的问题并修改。会困扰开发人员很长时间。因此，本文提供了一个如何从0创建一个CAP工程，打包部署以及本地调试的方法。可以通过这篇教程创建开发工程模板，节省开发时间。

本文主要为初学者提供一个创建最基础的MTA的CAP工程，涵盖了DB,SRV，Fiori，Managed Approuter等模块，可以成功打包部署。讲解分析创建过程中不同选项对最终工程的影响。对于资深CAP开发人员可以在本教程的基础上，个性化创建项目。

注意：BAS中CAP的版本会实时升级，本文写于2024-09，适用的版本版本如下

 

准备工作：请在SAP BTP中创建以下服务，并分配用户权限。

  • SAP Business Application Studio
  • SAP Build Workzone/ SAP Portal/ SAP BTP Fiori Launchpad
  • SAP HANA Cloud

1. SAP BAS开发环境准备

打开SAP BTP中的BAS服务，创建SAP Full Stack Cloud Application的开发空间。

2. 创建CAP工程

首先在BAS中通过模板创建一个CAP工程，选择CAP Project

 

选择SAP HANA Cloud以及Cloud Foundary: MTA Deployment.

虽然XSUAA等服务是CAP工程所需要的，但是如果在此勾选这些服务会影响后续的创建。所以在这一步无需选择。所需服务在后续步骤中添加。

 

选择Minimal Sample，程序会包含基本的DB module及SRV module

 

点击finish，等待程序创建结束

通过这种方法创建的MTA工程包含了DB和SRV module，并创建了实例代码，可以开始DB以及SRV的开发。在MTA.yaml中包含了srv及db module所需的资源，其中Hana的HDI实例可以根据项目需要修改名称。

  

 

 3. 在CAP工程中添加UI

首先添加Managed Approuter，在MTA.yaml文件上右键点击并选择Create MTA Moulde from Template，并选择Approuter Configuration

选择 Managed Approuter并且Add a UI 设置为yes

 

Managed Approuter 创建结束之后，会自动生成xsuaa的xs-security文件。

并且会自动更新MTA.yaml文件。添加Managed Approuter module以及所需services。

 

 

 

Managed approuter创建成功之后，添加Fiori Module，右键点击mta.yaml文件选择 Create module from template。选择SAP Fiori generator

 

 

 

 

 

 

Template Selection：

根据需求选择freestyle或者fiori element模板。本文选择List Report Page作为示例。

 

Data Source and Service Selection:

选择 Use a Local CAP Project, CAP_PILOT_PROJECT以及SRV提供的ODATA service

 

Entity Selection:

选择Entity。

 

Project Attributes：

输入fiori程序名字以及相关参数，注意一下两个选项需要设置成yes

 

Deploy Confuguration：

target需选择Cloud Foundry

Destination Name需要选择Local CAP Project API，选择这个选项会自动更新fiori中的xs app文件添加所需要的路由，后续会解释选择Local CAP Project API的原因。

 

Fiori Launchpad Configuration，

会更新manifest.json文件，添加BTP workzone需要的磁铁配置，如果Fiori Launchpad Configuration 选择No，会跳过此步骤，后续可以手动更新manifest文件添加所需配置

 

点击完成，创建fiori module，查看生成并修改了哪些文件

首先在deployment Configuration中选择了local cap Project API，会自动在xs-app.json中添加所需要的destination转发。如果在这一步骤选择了None，则会缺少相关的route配置。

缺少此route会导致BTP环境中 Fiori无法正确连接SRV 的OData。当运行Fiori应用时，会无法成功加载Metadata，显示404错误。因此当出现类似问题，可以首先查看xsapp文件排查错误原因。

如果在创建过程中destination name选择了None，后续需要手动添加此route。Destination名字可以在mta.yaml文件中找到。

在mta ymal文件中会添加fiori module并添加到app content中

 

至此一个涵盖了DB，SRV，Fiori的CAP工程就创建成功了。

 

4.打包部署到BTP平台

由于模板创建的工程缺少@sap/xssec，会导致SRV部署失败，所以首先在命令行执行npm install  @Sap/xssec

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

然后执行mbt build打包MTA工程

最后通过cf deploy 命令将程序部署到BTP平台。

部署成功，BTP中查看创建的instance以及部署的应用

 

Space中部署的db module 以及SRV module

 

点击application name打开Fiori程序，Fiori可以成功运行并且展示数据。

 

恭喜你！CAP创建及部署成功！

在这个项目的基础上，开始CAP开发之旅吧。

后续会有一篇文章讲解如何本地Debug 开发调试，敬请期待!

entry.title: Re: application to CF
entry.content: Unfortunately I forgot my crystal ball to help you ;-). You would need to provide more details if you expect anyone to help you.

entry.title: Re: BTP Build App
entry.content: With Build Apps you're building a web application. It can access files not directly. The browser can pass them to the Server when they where selected from the user.

entry.title: Re: Getting Cannot get/ error after deploying SAP BTP pro...
entry.content:
Hello everyone I found the solution to this error. I follow  this tutorial

 First use this command >cds add hana,mta,xsuaa,approuter --for production

I update my package.json by following this tutorial

https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/cap-service-deploy.html

entry.title: Re: Task 1 - Create CDS View Entity ( August Developer Challenge )
entry.content:
Data Preview:

Source Code:

entry.title: Re: price change getting error in MR21
entry.content:
Hi,

You need to check the configuration below
1) OKKP - Need to check currency settings for the controlling area
2) OBY6 - Check financial year, posting variants against company code
3) OX02 - Check company currency map properly
4) Check all configurations of the Path: Financial Accounting Global Settings (New)-> Ledgers -> Ledger

If any query please reply.

Regards,

entry.title: PDF file uploading by DTW in SAP B1
entry.content:
Dear All,

I tried the DTW OCRD Template. And add the following data : 

CardCodeCardNameAttachmentEntry
CardCodeCardNameAtcEntry
VP-10056Labmech Engineers\\172.16.3.13\SBO_Mail\Attachment

But after uploading the file, DTW giving error " 1 Update Failed VP-10056 CServiceData::SetPropertyValue failed; Value must be a whole number in property 'AttachmentEntry' of 'BusinessPartner'oBusinessPartners "

can anyone suggest the solution?

entry.title: Re: In-app purchases
entry.content:
Thank you so much. That was helpful!
Do you know if RevenueCat can be integarted with Sap Build Apps? (Appgyver)

@srose99

entry.title: Converting file from CRLF to LF in SAP Integration Suite
entry.content:
hi all,

We have a scenario where a .txt file (with multiple records)has to be placed in AL11 path of SAP for further processing. We noticed if we place Windows CRLF file then it produces a # at the end, for each record -when processing at SAP.

When changing the file manually to Unix LF, using notepad++ and placing it in AL11 , the processing happens smoothly. 

Can anyone suggest how we can achieve the LF file from CRLF using SAP Integration Suite??

Regards,

Smitha

entry.title: What scope item for Project system in Private cloud
entry.content:
Hi,

Can anyone please let me know scope items in Private cloud relevant for project system.

Thank You in advance,

BRM.

entry.title: Re: price change getting error in MR21
entry.content: Hi

entry.title: Re: price change getting error in MR21
entry.content: All configuration has been done again getting same issue please help for this issue

entry.title: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant
entry.content:
Expressing my interest here to join the digital skills initiative as a woman who seeks to officially break into tech.

I graduated BS Accountancy and have been using SAP in my career as an end user. I've gotten into an operations role where I was able to use SAP in operations and currently in an FP&A role where I utilize SAP to get the data I need from different departments.

My managers have been expressing their observation at how good I am at systems, and that maybe a career in digital transformation (or finance transformation) is something that I want to consider. Aside from being good in communication and business partnering, I think the most apt role that for me is to become a SAP consultant.

I've been looking through courses from different sites and on youtube, but I've read in the forums that courses in SAP academy would best help me achieve my goal, and earning a certification will give me a needed advantage to initiate the change.


In the future, I want to also get into business analytics and learn SAP Analytics Cloud as I already have domain expertise in my industry as an FP&A analyst.

Thanks and I look forward to upskilling with SAP academy and earning a certification with this initiative.

@David_Chaviano hope you can help with this. Thank you!

entry.title: Re: UI5 App with Standalone App Router gets: Timed out wa...
entry.content:
@ravijnj 

Yes we were able to solve the issue. At the end the request from cloud connector to the connectivity service IP addresses of the BTP wrer blocked by firewall settings. Only a set of IP addresses was allowed to be accessed by the cloud connector.

In fact this worked fine using the managed app router. But after deploying a standalone app including own app router, connectivity and destination service the ip address changed

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud Integration API - Collection of OData Clients using JS/TS and SAP Cloud SDK
entry.content:
Thank you for sharing, but I question the benefit of this? Why not use the published metadata on the Business Accelerator Hub and import the required modules you need for a specific project with the standard SAP Cloud SDK commands? Creating a custom module will require you (the author of this module) to maintain this every time SAP updates the API's or it's metadata, so now developers (like me) that would rely on this module will now rely on you to maintain this for us. Sometimes these standard packages contain small errors (e.g. when a new version of the cloud solution is released but the API documentation is not updated yet), so then you'd need access to the generated content in the project to make tweaks. This would be a bit more difficult if this generated content is hidden in a node module in which you're not supposed to make changes.

Keep on exploring though! CAP and Cloud SDK are magnificent frameworks!

entry.title: Re: Every single time I try to use Java it wont work
entry.content: Is this a real post? If it is, can you explain what you are talking about -- what tool, what error you get, screenshot?

entry.title: Re: How to migrate data with CDS view behaviour LTMOM

entry.title: Re: Task 4 - Implement Criticality feature and create an Object page
entry.content: Task - 4 - Criticality for Status

entry.title: Re: Tree Table not getting refreshed from Javascript
entry.content:
Found the solution...
Put resetChanges() before refreshing the model.

sap.ui.model.odata.v2.ODataModel - API Reference - Demo Kit - SAPUI5 SDK

oModel.resetChanges();

entry.title: Extract attributes from XML
entry.content:
I want to extract 2-attributes from XML. The names of attribute for extraction from this xml is -

ERPNumber, 

ProcurementUnit/UniqueName

UniqueName

 

My XML structures look like this -

<SimplifiedRequest>
<CancelExportRequest>
<OrdHeader_Item>
<item>
<ERPNumber>Y465643</ERPNumber>
<ProcurementUnit>
<UniqueName>6604</UniqueName>
</ProcurementUnit>
<UniqueName>EPT10053-V3</UniqueName>
<VersionNumber>3</VersionNumber>
<custom>
<CustomBoolean name="SuppressOrder">false</CustomBoolean>
<CustomString name="Test"/>
<CustomString name="SupplierID">AEQ600:0000399413</CustomString>
</custom>
</item>
</OrdHeader_Item>
</CancelExportRequest>
</SimplifiedRequest>

Can you help me here?

entry.title: Re: How to migrate data with CDS view behaviour LTMOM
entry.content: Hi Sybille, is there a way to simulate the data in the "Migrate Your Data" App?

entry.title: Recruiting Posting Job Board Setup: eFinancial
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

Just checking if anyone has experienced what we are experiencing in the lower instances.

We are implementing RP and one of the Job Boards is eFinancial.

We are unable to enter the values for the following fields:

Team ID

Brand ID
API Key
 
We are still able to add this Job Board even without filling out those fields. When we attempt to post on this Job Board on the lower instances, the job never posts, it just gets stuck to 'Processing'.
 
In setting up the Job Board, you will be given this message:
Credentials must be provided by eFinancialCareers.com.
SAP SuccessFactors Recruiting Posting will ask for authorisation to eFinancialCareers.com once you have completed your credential configuration steps.
We were thinking maybe once you set up the Job Board, an email gets sent out to eFinancial, they need to trigger something from their end so that in our Job Boards, the three fields will be automatically field.

We've reached out to them, they have not received any email. Has anyone experienced this or know how to resolve it?

entry.title: Re: Solman_setup 500 Internal Server Error
entry.content:
Hi Yongiun,

Check this SCN thread with same error message

https://community.sap.com/t5/application-development-discussions/500-sap-internal-server-error-error-access-using-a-zero∂object-reference-is/td-p/11462836

Regards

SS

entry.title: Re: Authentication issues when using RESTful api C# for 4.2 BusinessObjects
entry.content:
I found the issue. I noticed when replying previously that the 401 response included a referece to port 8080.

i.e this part:

Access-Control-Allow-Origin: http://servername:8080

I tried the below to see if I got a response in the browser:

http://servername:8080/biprws/logon/long

 It returned the same response when using 6405.

Changing the port to 8080 in the baseurl fixed the issue and returns the token. There must be a port redirection happening?

entry.title: Re: Noted Items in CDS view
entry.content: The required data is available with CDS view I_OperationalAcctgDocItem. See details in Accounting Document Item: Operational View

entry.title: Re: Entering batch at the time of Putaway - EWM
entry.content:
Hi,

"5) Why system is asking to put the Batch at the time of GR - ROD ? "

Because the batch is part of the stock information. You do notice that if you check your stock in ERP, the stock shows the different batches and their stock, yes? So in order to post GR, the system needs a batch.

Brgds

Juergen

---
All the above is no official SAP statement.

Want to learn EWM?
Check for EWM courses: https://training.sap.com/trainingpath/Applications-Extended+Warehouse+Management-EWM+in+S4HANA
Get a SAP Learning Hub Subscription: https://training.sap.com/learninghub

And it is EWM. NOT eWM - Duh!
And if your question includes the word "transfer order" - do NOT tag the question with Extended Warehouse Management!

entry.title: Re: Noted Items in CDS view
entry.content: The required data is available with CDS view I_OperationalAcctgDocItem. See details in Accounting Document Item: Operational View https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0fa84c9d9c634132b7c4abb9ffdd8f06/ca03bbfc892d4474be69934d6f5c783d.html?locale=en-US

entry.title: Getting error 'Could not find table/view XXXX in schema SAPABAP1’ in class method for table function
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I am using a CDS view in a class method for a table function . My CDS view is a consumption view with parameters. The table function and CDS view are both activated but when I try to activate the class/method it gives me the below error :-

‘Could not find table/view ZETDCNVW1360002 in schema SAPABAP1’

Method Snapshot

 

Any inputs why would this be happening?

entry.title: Re: Will firewall affects while connecting to database ta...
entry.content: Consider using the cloud connector to enable Cloud Integration to connect to your database. Then they don't need to get a public IP or port.

entry.title: Re: Will firewall affects while connecting to database ta...
entry.content:
Hi Yash,

On those two different DB servers firewall on? if it is yes, you have to take help from network security team to fulfill your requirement.

Regards

SS

entry.title: Re: Delete logs from table DBTABLOG with report RSTBPDEL
entry.content: Hi could you share the full dumps as text file attachment

entry.title: Re: Standard CDS view as app
entry.content:
Hi Kristian2,

kindly try to expose the cds views via app Custom Analytical Queries. Details you can follow the link below:
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0f69f8fb28ac4bf48d2b57b9637e81fa/33f3a229ecfe438ca6b2053c48b4e1bb.html?q=custom%20analytical%20query&locale=en-US

and you can publish it as an application.

 

Best regards,

Angela

entry.title: ECP can host 2 combined payrolls Egypt &USA,
entry.content:
Hi  Experts,

I would like to know if the ECP payroll can host combined payroll for Egypt & USA in the same system, to run both together.

Regards

Mira Atef

entry.title: Re: Define Parameter Catalog for Subprocedure Determinati...
entry.content:
Hi,

Before you can add a custom field to the parameter catalog, the custom field must exist in the extensibility business context "Sales: Pricing Communication Head".

This blog gives you a comprehensive explanation: Get to know more on Modular Pricing Procedures in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition

Best Regards,
Fancy

entry.title: Re: ChaRM - Cannot select single transport for scheduled import
entry.content: An import for urgent changes will be triggered with the status change 'Import to Production' in UC. If you want to import via the task list, you need to change the import strategy.

entry.title: Re: ChaRM - Cannot select single transport for scheduled...
entry.content: An import for urgent changes will be triggered with the status change 'Import to Production' in UC. If you want to import via the task list, you need to change the import strategy.

entry.title: FM Budget Exceed at the time of MIRO
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I am reaching out to seek your insights regarding a budget check issue encountered during the MIRO process for asset transactions.

In the previous fiscal year (2024), our client successfully completed the PR, PO, and GR for certain assets. However, when attempting to execute MIRO in 2025 fiscal year, the system prompts for budget availability. This shows that previous budget is fully consumed.

Interestingly, it seems to affect only 2 to 3 specific cases. Furthermore, a review of the budget consumption report from the previous year indicates that the total budget amount is still showing as available, suggesting that no amounts have actually been consumed.

appreciate any guidance or insights on how to resolve this inconsistency.

entry.title: Re: How to implement Fiscal Year Variant SZ?
entry.content: Please also refer to "Setting Up an Alternative Fiscal Year Variant" (https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0fa84c9d9c634132b7c4abb9ffdd8f06/b069d5700c464155b0ef2e077027e7ee.html?q=fiscal%20year%20variant&locale=en-US)

entry.title: Import ABAP Dictionary Structure doesn't Create CRUD-Methods in SEGW
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I got a problem that if I import a new structure into my data model and generate the runtime object, it doesn't create methods like CREATE, UPDATE, GET_ENTITY, GET_ENTITYSET etc. in the DPC_EXT. Does anyone know, why that is?

I'm in the transaction SEGW and created a new project for an App Extension. Thanks for the communities help in advance!

Cheers

entry.title: Re: report of slow moving item
entry.content:
T-Code MC46

In initial screen define selection with parameters like plant, period to analyze and tick check box 'stock value'

execute.

Click double/tripple line to view status with values.

Regards.

entry.title: Recruiting Posting Job Board Setup: SeekSG
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We've set up the SeekSG Job Board in the lower instances (Test). When we attempt to post to it, we first fill out the fields. However, there are two fields, specifically for SeekSG that 1) seems to load when you click on it but never shows any selection 2) can be filled out but as soon as you click outside of the field, it defaults back to being blank.

Has anyone experienced this before?

entry.title: Re: 3 Decimal places for Data Migration in S4 Public cloud
entry.content:
Hello,

Thank you very much for your question.

Here's the information you're looking for:

Additional Information About XML Template Files:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/d5699934e7004d048c4801b552f3b013/ca2e848ff03e478db96d7dfa9dd800f3.html?locale=en-US

"Number

  • Enter numbers with decimals in the relevant country-specific format, for example 12.34 (United States) or 12,34 (Germany). For fields with decimal places, the length includes the number of decimal places (if required), for example if the information for the column states Length: 8, Decimals: 3, then a number such as 12345.678 is permitted. Note that decimal places are not mandatory. In is this example, you can specify a whole number up to length 8 without decimal places, for example ‘1’. This number would be set to ‘1.000’ internally. For negative numbers, ensure that a minus sign (‘-‘) directly precedes the number, for example ‘-100’.

    Note that the maximum field length supported by Microsoft Excel is 15 digits (including decimals). If you have longer numbers, use the option for transferring data to S/4HANA using staging tables. For more information, see SAP Knowledge Base Article 2718516"

    Kind regards,

    Henrike

     

     

    .

entry.title: Re: PDF file uploading by DTW in SAP B1
entry.content:
Hi,

AttachmentEntry accepts integer value only. Also, it will accept the existing value from OATC table.

It is required to have the entry related to VP-10056.PDF in the OATC table first. Then only, it can be referred to attach.

Hope it helps!

Kind regards,

ANKIT CHAUHAN

SAP Business One Support

entry.title: Email notification to employee if his final performance rating is Not meeting expectation
entry.content:
Hello Experts 

We have 2,4,6 and 8 months performance evaluation form for new comer. we want to send email notification to the respective employee if his final performance rating is NME (Non Meeting Expectation) once his performance form is sent to completion stage by his manager.

Please guide how this is achievable

Regards

entry.title: Re: Do Virtual Tables support ST_POINT or ST_GEOMETRY
entry.content:
ok create a view in source system to convert the GEO to text.

create or replace view MYSCHEMA.MYVIEW as ( select
GEOFIELD.ST_AsText() as GEOFIELDTEXT
from "MYSCHEMA.TABLE_WITH_GEO" ) ;

in target system create VT to connect to view

Once the VT is created

Then select GEOFIELDTEXT.ST_GeomFromText() as GEOFIELD  from VT 

The Geo types have been converted back.

entry.title: Re: renumber g/l account
entry.content: Thanks for your question. It has been forwarded to the experts.

entry.title: What’s New in SAP Central Business Configuration 2406 FD3
entry.content:
With the latest feature delivery (FD3) for SAP Central Business Configuration, exciting new features are available. This blog post will provide you with a comprehensive overview of these features.

Enhanced Transparency with Deployment Descriptions

In the Deployment Target app, if you have a 3-system landscape, we’ve added a new column to display deployment descriptions. These are the descriptions that you can enter optionally when you carry out the Confirm Scope and Organizational Structure Phase Is Completed milestone. They’re now conveniently visible in the Deployment History table for easier access.

Improved User Experience with Column-Level Filters

Enhancing the user experience even further, we’ve enabled table filters at the column level. In addition to sorting table entries, you can now apply filters at the column level, making it easier to work with tables, particularly for those users working on mobile devices. This feature is available in the tables of the Deployment Target app, the Team section of the Settings app, and the Change Logs app.

Screenshot of the table filtering capabilities at column level and the Deployment Description column

For a quick summary, check out this video about What's New in 2406 & FD1–FD3. Don’t forget to also use the What’s New Viewer on the SAP Help Portal, which provides further details and links about all the recent changes.

That wraps up the new features this time. Make sure to follow the SAP Central Business Configuration tag or follow this dedicated blog collection to stay up to date with the latest blog posts.

Do you have an idea on how to further enhance SAP Central Business Configuration? Go to the SAP Customer Influence portal to submit an improvement request or vote for the ideas of others. This helps us understand your requirements even better and prioritize our work accordingly. We’re looking forward to your improvement requests.

entry.title: I want to connect SAC to my on premise SAP HANA database
entry.content: I want to connect SAC to my on premise SAP HANA database, I have filled all the necessary details but still not connecting it is showing error . Please help if any one knows any solution

entry.title: Re: How to get the MC.9 report data for prior periods?
entry.content:
Hi,

In MC.9 Initial screen, in put target periods in 'period to analyze' fields. 

define other selections like plant, SLOC, material groups e.t.c as required.

Execute.

To see time series of stocks, place cursor in valuated stock header and click time series button.

Your report will output

Regards.

ookocharlis@gmail.com

entry.title: Re: SAP GUI for Java 8.10 on the Horizon
entry.content: Looking forward to the release! Please comment or update the post as soon as it's out.

entry.title: Re: Introducing SAP Analytics Cloud, add-in for Microsoft PowerPoint
entry.content:
Hi @sabine_krueger1 

1. that widget texts are often cut scalable and are often cut off 

2. that that part of widgets (e.g. legends) are missing.

  [ASHOK]: Widgets are displayed based on the size available in the add-in. Users can expand the add in to see the whole content. we are also evaluating an option to provide an option to add widget in Edit button in the upcoming releases. 

Regards,
Ashok R

entry.title: Re: Best approach to compare HANA Tables
entry.content:
Hi Kiran - I propose you model a Calculation View with calculated columns which do the calculation KF T1 - KF T2. This new column could be evaluated on a more aggregated level and you could analyze in more detail only if there are results <> 0.

KR Frank

entry.title: Re: SAP Community - Where are you from?
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I'm from Izmir, Turkey. I have been reading the wonderful blogs here for a couple of months and have enjoyed being a part of this friendly community. Now, I'd love to contribute some blogs of my own.

Best regards,
Beykan

entry.title: How do I trigger a User Defined Object in PostTransactionNotice
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I have created a UDO and linked UDT's to it. The example below shows and extract of the lines table:

I have created the following script in PostTransactionNotice to update an external table with changes to the status. Running the query on it's own works perfectly, but it does not trigger in PostTransactionNotice.
Do I need to change something for the PostTransactionNotice to read the Objecttype?
IF @object_TYPE = 'MRMSCC' and @transaction_type = 'U'
BEGIN
Update [MRM_CC].[dbo].[ServiceCallInformation]
set ItemStatus = UF.U_ScStatus
From
(select Code,LineID,U_ScStatus from [SAP_CCR].[dbo].[@_MRMCSCLN]
where U_ScStatus = 'Closed') UF
where Id = UF.LineId
and UF.Code = @List_of_cols_val_tab_del
END

entry.title: Re: Dump Error Raise_Exception CNTL_ERROR
entry.content: Hlo Maria,                                                                                                                                            Can you share your code???.

entry.title: Custom CDS View for Customer and Contact
entry.content:
Hi - I am currently creating a customer CDS view for API extraction. I want to return the Corporate Account to Contact relationship easily. I am currently using I_MKT_ContactRelationData & I_Mkt_Contact but it's not showing the relationship if I filter on the corporate account.

Is there a different data source we should use?

Cheers,

Marc

entry.title: Export contract net value
entry.content:
Hi, I am newbie using sap and at work we use Launchpad.

I would like to create a report and for it’s required to pull the contracts net value and to avoid doing a manual job of checking every single contract I was wondering if there’s any transaction that could do the job.

 

can anyone help me?

 

thanks a million

entry.title: Re: Using API_CV_ATTACHMENT_SRV/AttachmentContentSet
entry.content: Hi, I have recently been struggling to get tis API to work in SAP Cloud Integration. Eventually for me the solution was to put only the content of the attachment as a binary string in the message payload and to send the other information (BusinessObjectTypeName, LinkedSAPObjectKey, Slug, Content-Type) as headers. Hope it will work for you too...

entry.title: Re: Getting error 'Could not find table/view XXXX in schema SAPABAP1’ in class method for table func
entry.content:
There might be some hickup / inconsistency in the creating / editing of the View ...
The following is an Extract from SAP-Note "2535951 - FAQ: SAP HANA Users and Schemas" :

Error message
[259]: invalid table name: Could not find table/view <object_name> in schema <schema_name>

Details
This error indicates that the object wasn't found in the relevant schema. Double-check if the object exists in the used schema and provide an explicit schema name if required. Also make sure that sufficient authorizations exist to access the referenced objects.

Check in your Backend via Trx."SE14" if the View exists, is active etc ...
If a problem is indicated, you might be able to use Prgr. "RUTDDLSACT" to regenerate / activate the "Z"-View?

Hope this helps

Nic T.

entry.title: Re: Talent Intelligence Hub - Myths and Misconceptions - Part 2 (Migration)
entry.content:
@cstal you're very welcome! 😊

Regarding your questions:

  1. We are currently in the middle of a transition from the legacy skill recommendation engine that was only available to early adopters and is no longer available / supported to a brand new skill recommendation engine which is supposed to be available as of the 2H 2024 release. That new AI-powered skill recommendation feature will be a Premium AI feature and so it will consume credits. However, you can enable and use Growth Portfolio without this feature.
  2. and also 3.: The redesigned Growth Portfolio requires TIH, OMP and CWS to be in place. None of these 3 prerequisites are optional. If your customer currently doesn't have CWS or OMP, they can't use the new version of GP. There is currently no configuration option for disabling certain sections in GP like the current and future role related one. The whole point of the new experience for CWS / GP is that we want to provide a one stop shop for everything related to an employees skills & competencies, their career aspirations and plans and related growth opportunities.

My personal recommendation is to look into the extent to which your customer would benefit from OMP. Do they have on-the-job development offerings, so they could benefit from the assignments feature? Do they use SF Learning or Recruiting or Mentoring? Once they have at least 2-3 types of development opportunities in place that would feed into OMP it makes sense to have a conversation about OMP with them.

entry.title: Facing 401 unauthorized error while trying post data to CPI via SAP API Management
entry.content:
Hi,

I am trying to send request to SAP CPI via API Management.

But I am facing "401 Unauthorized" error while connecting to CPI though I have maintained correct credential in KeyValue Map. (CSRF token is unchecked in CPI HTTP adapter)

Using same credentials I am able to call CPI endpoint directly from Postman send data successfully.

I tried to test from both API Management and Postman(by calling API endpoint) but no luck. Also tried removing all the other policies.

Can someone help me understand if we have to do any kind of whitelisting or additional roles to be added to call CPI from APIM?

Below is the screen from APIM Debug :

Thanks in advance.

Regards,

Madhura
